reasoning: The FRED statistical model predicts a short-term decrease from the recent spike (0.75 to 0.84 in late October/early November), giving a 0.35 probability of the spread being higher than 0.84 on November 16. Spreads are currently near historical minimums, but short-term momentum from the recent spike will likely reverse as market stress subsides. Over the medium to longer term (December and February), my uncertainty increases, pulling the probabilities closer to 0.50. Spreads face asymmetric risks (they cannot drop much below 0.70 but can widen significantly), slightly elevating the odds of an increase over longer horizons compared to the short-term mean reversion.
